title: No client-side routing ÔÇö navigation between application screens is impossible
body:
## Issue description

The frontend has no client-side routing library. `react-router-dom` (or any equivalent) is not listed in `client/package.json`. The React app renders a single component directly from `App.tsx` with no `<Routes>` / `<Route>` structure:

```tsx
// client/src/App.tsx
export function App() {
  return (
    <ThemeProvider>
      <SkipLink />
      <Home />
    </ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

As a result:
- The navigation links in `Home.tsx` (`href="#groups"`, `href="#events"`) are anchor hash-links that lead nowhere.
- No URL-based navigation is possible.
- Deep-linking to any feature (e.g. `/groups/123/events/456`) is impossible.
- The browser back/forward buttons cannot be used to navigate within the app.

Without routing the application cannot be structured into the screens required by the product design (`docs/architecture/overview.md`).

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

- Install `react-router-dom` v6+ (or a comparable routing library) in the client.
- Define a route tree that maps URL paths to screen components:
  - `/` or `/login` Ôćĺ authentication landing
  - `/groups` Ôćĺ groups list
  - `/groups/:groupId` Ôćĺ group detail
  - `/groups/:groupId/events/new` Ôćĺ event creation / capture
  - `/events/:eventId` Ôćĺ event detail
  - `/events/:eventId/chat` Ôćĺ event chat
  - `/events/:eventId/checklist` Ôćĺ event checklist
  - `/events/:eventId/location` Ôćĺ event location
  - `/profile` Ôćĺ user profile
- Wrap the route tree in an authentication guard so unauthenticated users are redirected to the login page for any protected route.
- The `client/Dockerfile` nginx configuration already includes an SPA fallback (`try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html`) so deep-linking will work with the existing nginx setup.
- All navigation within the app must use `<Link>` / `useNavigate` ÔÇö no `<a href>` elements that cause full-page reloads.

## Additional notes

- The nginx SPA fallback in `client/Dockerfile` is already correct and requires no changes.
- Route params (`groupId`, `eventId`) must be validated before use in API calls to prevent injection attacks.
- Accessibility: focus management on route transitions must be handled (e.g. move focus to the main heading after navigation).
- The `SkipLink` component already targets `#main-content`; every page component must render a `<main id="main-content">` wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `react-router-dom` (v6+) is installed as a client dependency.
- [ ] A route tree is defined covering all core application paths.
- [ ] Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to the login/auth flow.
- [ ] All in-app navigation uses React Router `<Link>` or `useNavigate`; no raw `<a href>` for internal links.
- [ ] Deep-linking works: loading `/groups/123` in a fresh browser tab renders the correct screen.
- [ ] Browser back/forward navigation works correctly.
- [ ] Route parameters are validated before use.
- [ ] Tests cover route rendering and redirect behaviour for protected routes.
- [ ] CI passes.
